text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User. text_content: For the most rapid regulatory clearances internationally, the product name on the submission documents needs to match the name to be cleared and used in marketing the device. Upgrades and Conversions: Upgrades from a previous version to HAL 4.0 can be made in some countries where HAL 4.0 is cleared. The details of which countries allow this will be detailed later in the project. For China, the system updates cannot engage any re-labelling activities, the information on device label should not be changed, such as manufacturing date, model name, NMPA license number etc. . Conversions from Halcyon to Ethos radiotherapy sytem can be made in some countries. Conversions involve changes to the covers, installing and enabling the Ethos TPS (On-couch session manager or OSM) function and changing the nameplate to include “Ethos Radiotherapy System” while manufacturing date and serial number remain the same. The UDI will be updated to describe the changed model. Regulatory Plan Revision Doc Number

title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.2 Scope
text_content: This Clinical Evaluation Plan covers HAL treatment platform, version 4.0. Clinical evaluation is an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device. It is first performed during the conformity assessment process leading to the marketing of a medical device and then repeated periodically in the post- marketing setting as new clinical safety and performance information about the device is obtained from surveillance programs and other sources. The clinical evaluation report and the clinical data on which it is based serve as the clinical evidence that supports the general safety and performance requirements for the device. The clinical evaluation report is part of the technical documentation of the device. title: 3.2.1 Scope, Schedule and Budget Control
text_content: Scope control will consist of managing requirement changes, enhancements, feature requests and scope changes.  Each will be controlled as outlined below. Requirement changes: If all of these conditions are met:  The changes do not impact the essential functionality and/or user experience of the device  The changed do not impact the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Then the changes will be managed through the document control process using Review Report Forms (LL40). If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. Enhancements and Feature Requests: Any requirement changes to implement additional features or enhancements, after Elaboration Phase, will be collected as TFS backlog items reporting to an Enhancement EPIC. source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User.  Activities performed per appropriate STI’s.  Validate and update STI documentation  Findings are recorded in a Test Report and issues submitted. HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 Validation Plan

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. Affect patient safety, e.g. adopt new software safety standards and add alerting information to user interface The calculation speed improvement of existing core algorithm, reconfigurable of clinical workflow (i.e. user could keep original clinical workflow), literal change on user interface are not considered as significant change unless it has impact on medical device safety and effectiveness.
